title: Forty-Nine Reasons
theme: John Patterson builds a Trophy-winning cushion with a historic 49-point round at Praia D'El Rey, then defends it through a final-day wobble — all amid a tournament of extravagant blowups on Seve's last design and the windswept dunes next door.
opening_hook: Open on Patterson signing for 49 points on the Saturday at Praia D'El Rey — the 2nd-best round in TEG history — then rewind to the Friday carnage at Royal Óbidos that made the cushion necessary.
foreshadow: Patterson's sextuple bogey 10 at Royal Óbidos's 4th in R1 — hinting that this Trophy will be won despite the wreckage, not without it
Alex Baker's bottomless R1 capacity for damage at Óbidos, foreshadowing the twin PBs he would later post when the wind got into him
David Mullin seizing the Gross lead at R1 H3 and never letting go — a quiet procession beneath the noise

competitions.name: The Trophy (Stableford)
competitions.how: After a scratchy, blowup-pocked 38 at Royal Óbidos left him a point behind Jon Baker, Patterson seized the outright lead at the very first hole of R2 at Praia D'El Rey and never gave it back. The decisive blow was his 49-point Saturday — the 2nd-best round in TEG history — built around a 17-point burst through holes 9–13 and a closing flourish of 14 points over the last four. A patchy 34 at Óbidos in R3 trimmed the lead from 10 to 3, and Alex Baker briefly drew level at the 14th of R4, but Patterson's 47 on the final day reopened the gap to a comfortable 6. Final margin: 168 to Alex Baker's 162.

competitions.name: The Green Jacket (Gross)
competitions.how: A procession. Mullin grabbed the outright Gross lead at the 3rd hole of R1, surrendered it briefly to Jon Baker around the turn, and reclaimed it for good by the 18th green of Royal Óbidos. From there the gap only widened — 2, then 7, then 7, then 10 — as he posted gross rounds of 15, 14, 22 and 16 over par. A quintuple at the 10th in R3 and a quad at the 1st in R4 were the only real scares, both swiftly absorbed. Wire-to-wire from R1 H18 onwards; final margin 10 over Jon Baker.

competitions.name: The Wooden Spoon
competitions.how: Bottom of the pile from the 15th hole of R1 onwards and never out of last place at any round-end thereafter. The damage was done in a five-hole haemorrhage to close Royal Óbidos in R1 — 16 shots leaked, including a quintuple at the 15th — leaving him with a 23-point round he could never out-run. Rounds of 30, 37 and 37 followed, and even a brave 16-point burst from the 11th–15th in R4 (his best stretch of the week) was nowhere near enough. Final tally: 127 points.

headline_candidates: Big Numbers on the Big Course
Jon Baker Leads, Everyone Else Bleeds
rounds.angle: Royal Óbidos — Ballesteros's final design — extracts blood from everyone. Jon Baker emerges with a one-point lead, but the headlines belong to the carnage: Patterson's 10, Alex Baker's 11, Gregg's serial wreckage, and Neumann's closing five-hole collapse that books his seat at the bottom for the duration.

Praia D'El Rey turns into Patterson's stage. He takes the outright lead at the 1st, rides a 17-point surge from the 9th, closes with 14 points over the last four, and walks off with 49 — the 2nd-best round in TEG history. Alex Baker quietly matches his personal best (44) in the same wind. Lead out to 10.

headline_candidates: Back to Óbidos, Back to Earth
rounds.chosen_headline: Back to Óbidos, Back to Earth
rounds.angle: Seve's course bites Patterson back: a 34, with quads at the 12th and 18th, shrinks the cushion from 10 to 3. Jon Baker rallies with a hot front nine but fades on the back. Mullin's Jacket procession barely flinches despite a quintuple at the 10th.

The only real Sunday drama: Alex Baker, posting his second 44-point PB of the week, hauls level with Patterson at the 14th. Patterson responds, posts 47, and the gap reopens to 6. Gregg adds an 11 at the 7th to his collection; Mullin closes out the Jacket by 10.

players.arc: Survives a sextuple bogey on the opening day, then detonates a 49-point Saturday that reshapes the tournament; weathers a final-day level-up by Alex Baker and lifts the Trophy by 6 — his first, and the highest Stableford total in TEG history to date.
Seizes the Gross lead at the 3rd hole of R1 and conducts a near-untroubled wire-to-wire procession to a 10-shot Jacket win.

Runner-up on the Trophy with twin personal-best 44s in the Praia wind, briefly level with Patterson on the final back nine — all of it punctuated by a catalogue of blowups including an 11 at Óbidos's 14th.
Leads after R1, runs hot through the opening five holes of R3, and settles for Jacket runner-up behind the immovable Mullin.
Files a sextuple bogey in three of the four rounds — 9, 10, 11 — a TEG 9 highlight reel of its own.
Bottom of the pile from R1 H15 onwards; a five-hole collapse to close Óbidos sealed a Spoon he never threatened to escape, despite a spirited R4 mid-round flurry.

venue_notes: TEG's 3rd visit to the Lisbon Coast (2016), alternating Royal Óbidos and Praia D'El Rey. Frame Óbidos as Seve Ballesteros's final design — dramatic water, championship teeth, and (this week) a near-bottomless appetite for double-digit scores; it's now the 3rd TEG round here. Praia D'El Rey — parkland out, links-in-the-dunes back — hosts its 3rd and 4th TEG rounds, and proves the more generous of the two: Patterson's 49 and both of Alex Baker's PB 44s come there. Sprinkle the venue colour into the round openings, not the lede.
